data.content: The file is generated successfully but never added to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at **evaluation time** (before targets run), so the generated file is invisible to the compiler. The fix is to explicitly add it to `Compile` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs before `CoreCompile` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild globs expand at evaluation time, before any targets run, so the generated file isn't picked up automatically.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `<Compile>` item group within the target, and also to `<FileWrites>` so it gets cleaned properly.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Add the generated file to compilation and register for Clean -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
      <FileWrites Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds now. Here's the diagnosis and fix:

**Problem:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes `GeneratedInfo.cs` to `obj/`, but never adds it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild implicit globs (`**/*.cs`) expand at **evaluation time** — before any targets execute — so the file is invisible to the compiler even though it exists on disk.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target to:
1. `<Compile Include="...">` — tells the compiler about the generated file
2. `<FileWrites Include="...">` — registers it for cleanup by `dotnet clean`
